body_txt: One of the potential risks of throwing a party is the possibility of wanted guests refusing to come for one reason or another. Sometimes a wanted guest might not show up or, even worse, a party might be empty or nearly empty because of several guests refusing to come. One, somewhat morally questionable, solution to this dilemma is to simply force someone to come to the party. The type of party can be anything such as a house party, a mixer, a birthday party, a wedding, or any other party. There are multiple ways someone can force someone else to come to a party; one method is to threaten to do something bad to someone such as physical violence, exposing a secret, or spreading rumors about someone . Another method is to annoy and harass someone until they agree to come to the party. Some hosts might even go so far as to outright kidnap people to be their guests, and ensure they don't leave, either by threats or restraints. Another variant is to stop someone currently attending from leaving a party, often through the aforementioned methods. The reason why someone would resort to this can vary. Someone who wants to invite someone to a party to humiliate them might do this to ensure that their victim can't avoid humiliation by not coming. Someone who has few, if any, friends might do this because they know any party they threw wouldn't have many/any members. Another variant is for someone to be forced to attend a party by someone other than the one hosting the party. A common example being one's parents, who often force their child to go to a party they don't want to attend for various reasons, such as being nice, wanting them to socialize, and seeing it as a way to be friends with the one throwing the party. Compare Captive Date . See also Captive Audience for when people are forced to watch a performance. Could overlap with Not a Party Person if an invitee doesn't like parties. Contrast Uninvited to the Party . Not to be confused with Required Party Member , which is about parties in video games. See also Nasty Party and A Fête Worse than Death for parties with the intention to kill someone.
